group: FIVE. Taking Leave, Continuing Studies, Withdrawal, and Dismissal

provisions.content: If a student is unable to attend classes for more than 3 weeks due to valid reasons, they shall submit a request for a semester or academic year leave of absence electronically to the unit responsible for academic activities.

The duration of a single leave of absence is up to 2 semesters. The total duration of leave shall not exceed 2 years. Periods of active military service, pregnancy and childcare leave, or cases where study is impossible due to a medical board decision shall not be counted towards the total duration.
Semester leave shall be calculated and granted from the beginning of the respective semester.
Based on the decision of the unit responsible for academic activities to grant leave, it shall be formalized by an order from the Director of the component school.
The period during which a student was on leave shall not be counted towards the duration of study.
Leave of less than 21 days shall be requested in writing or electronically to the instructor teaching the course, and the instructor shall make the decision.

A student whose leave period has ended shall submit a request to continue their studies at least 14 days before the start of classes. This shall be formalized by an order from the Director of the component school, and the student's status shall be activated.
A student who has exceeded their leave period and failed to return to study shall be transferred to 'inactive' student status.

A student shall be dismissed from NUM by an order of the President of NUM for the following reasons: failure to participate in training for 4 semesters; GPA below 0.7 for 3 consecutive semesters; remaining in the general foundation program for 4+ semesters without selecting a major; confirmed cases of academic dishonesty; intentionally allowing others to cheat; intentional alteration or deletion of information in the NUM information system; forging NUM documents; committing serious ethical violations; or committing a crime.

A student who did not take a leave of absence during the given semester and failed to study any courses without a specific reason, or received a GPA of 0, shall be considered as 'not participated in training'.
At the end of each semester, warning information shall be delivered to students who are approaching the conditions for dismissal.
